name: BYJU'S
description: Once valued at $22B with $5.5B raised, BYJU'S collapsed under aggressive acquisitions, financial mismanagement, and allegations of siphoning $533M from a $1.2B loan.
story: BYJU'S was founded in Bangalore in 2011 and became India's most valuable startup, raising $5.5 billion and reaching a $22 billion peak valuation. The company used AI for personalized learning, adaptive assessments, and content recommendations across its suite of K-12 education products. The collapse was spectacular. BYJU'S acquired 19 companies for $3.63 billion in a rapid expansion spree that proved unsustainable. Allegations surfaced that the company siphoned $533 million from a $1.2 billion term loan. Founder Byju Raveendran was ousted in February 2024, and India's National Company Law Tribunal initiated insolvency proceedings in July 2024. The post-pandemic return to physical schools accelerated the decline. At its peak, BYJU'S employed tens of thousands; its fall remains the largest edtech collapse in history.

sources.title: The rise and fall of BYJU'S
